title: First-baby starter kit for a no-nursery apartment — ready in 2 weeks
summary: Two weeks is tight, so I built this around what you'll genuinely need in the first 3-4 months — not a full nursery you don't have room for. I assumed mid-tier means "safe, well-reviewed, not luxury" and prioritized apartment-friendly footprints (bedside bassinet over crib first, compact stroller, slim diaper pail). Total lands around $1,495 — say the word if you want to swap the travel system for a cheaper combo or add a glider/rocker.

sections.name: Sleep setup (apartment-friendly)
sections.purpose: A safe sleep space that fits next to your bed and grows with baby — no nursery required.

products.name: BassiNest Swivel Sleeper Premiere
products.why_this: Since you have no nursery, this bedside bassinet swivels over your bed so you can soothe and feed without getting up. The narrow base fits in small apartment bedrooms where a full crib won't.

products.name: SNIGLAR Crib + KRUMMELUR Mattress
products.why_this: Solid wood crib for ~$80 plus a foam mattress for ~$50 — you'll move baby into this around 4-6 months when they outgrow the bassinet. Buy directly from IKEA; it's the best value crib that meets US safety standards.

products.name: SleepSack Swaddle (3-pack, Newborn)
products.why_this: Safer than loose blankets (which aren't allowed in the crib anyway) and easier than learning to wrap a muslin swaddle at 3am with a screaming newborn.

sections.name: Feeding
sections.purpose: Covers both bottle and breastfeeding paths so you're not scrambling if plans change.

products.name: Natural Flow Anti-Colic Options+ Bottles (Newborn Set)
products.why_this: The vented design genuinely reduces gas and spit-up — pediatricians recommend them constantly. The newborn set gives you bottles, slow-flow nipples, and the cleaning brush in one box.

products.name: Original Nursing Pillow
products.why_this: Whether you're breastfeeding or bottle-feeding, this saves your arms and back during the 8-12 daily feeds. Also doubles as tummy-time prop later.

products.name: Bottle Drying Rack
products.why_this: Apartment kitchens don't have counter space for a giant grass drying rack. This one is compact and the pegs hold bottle parts upright so they actually dry.

sections.name: Diapering station
sections.purpose: A portable changing setup since you don't have a dedicated nursery room.

products.name: Peanut Changer
products.why_this: Wipeable, waterproof pad that sits on top of any dresser — no sheets to wash, no contour pad covers. Perfect for converting a regular dresser into a changing station in your bedroom or living room.

products.name: Steel Diaper Pail
products.why_this: Uses regular trash bags (not proprietary refills like the Diaper Genie) and the steel construction actually contains the smell — critical in an apartment where you can't tuck it away in a separate room.

products.name: Baby Grow Smart Changing Pad + Scale
products.why_this: Skip — the Keekaroo above is the better pick for apartment living. (Listed at $0 so it doesn't affect the budget.)

sections.name: Going out
sections.purpose: Car seat + stroller combo that works for apartment storage and city errands.

products.name: PIPA RX Infant Car Seat + Base
products.why_this: Lightweight (under 8 lbs) which matters when you're carrying it up apartment stairs, and it clicks into most stroller frames. Highest safety ratings and one of the easiest bases to install correctly.

products.name: MINU V2 Stroller
products.why_this: Folds one-handed to a compact size that fits in apartment closets and car trunks, and the Nuna PIPA RX clicks directly onto it with adapters. A full travel system without the bulk of a Vista.

sections.name: Bath, health, and the small things you'll need on day 1
sections.purpose: The non-glamorous essentials that you'll reach for in the first 48 hours home.

products.name: Grooming Kit + NoseFrida + Thermometer Bundle
products.why_this: Nail clippers, the snot sucker every parent swears by, and a rectal thermometer (the only kind pediatricians trust for newborns). You'll use all three in week one.

products.name: Tub Soft Infant Bath
products.why_this: Folds flat to hang on a hook — critical in an apartment bathroom with no storage. Fits in any sink so you don't need a full tub setup for the first few months.

next_steps: Install the car seat base this week and get it inspected at a local fire station or CPST — most are free and they'll catch any install errors before baby arrives.
Wash all clothes, swaddles, and crib sheets in fragrance-free detergent (Dreft or All Free Clear) before first use.
Stock the diaper caddy and set up a small 'station' on every floor/room you spend time in — you'll thank yourself at 3am.
